title: rubus argenteus var. tourletii
intro: Rubus argenteus var. tourletii, also known as Tourlet's silver-bracted raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Rubus argenteus species, characterized by its silvery-gray leaf undersides and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Rubus argenteus var. tourletii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, with the leaves and roots being used to create a range of medicinal products.
